arts.ttl: Fans worry over latest Haaland twist
arts.sum: The injury occurred as Haaland attempted to shake off Cook, but as he fell, his left ankle twisted awkwardly under the defender’s body. His ankle has long been a troublesome area, and despite his efforts to continue, he was forced to exit after receiving medical attention.

arts.ttl: Pep Guardiola claims Man City rediscovered their 'heart and soul' in Bournemouth win - as he singles out one star among 'legendary' squad
arts.sum: JAMES SHARPE: Pep Guardiola believes his legendary Manchester City players rediscovered their 'heart and soul' to secure their place in a seventh successive FA Cup semi.

arts.ttl: Bournemouth players must put FA Cup heartbreak behind them and push for Europe, insists manager Andoni Iraola
arts.sum: Iraola says Bournemouth must pick themselves up quickly from the disappointment of a 'missed opportunity' to reach their first-ever FA Cup semi-final if they are to fulfil their Euro dreams.

arts.ttl: Forest v Man City, Palace v Villa - semi-final draw
arts.sum: The full FA Cup semi-final draw, with ties taking place on the weekend of Saturday, 26 April.

arts.ttl: Major Erling Haaland injury update given as worrying Man City news emerges
arts.sum: Manchester City forward Erling Haaland suffered a nasty-looking ankle injury against Bournemouth, forcing him to be substituted

arts.ttl: Star players make step up, says Guardiola
arts.sum: Manchester City's "legendary" players find their heart after coming from behind to beat Bournemouth 2-1 in the FA Cup, says boss Pep Guardiola.

arts.ttl: Last shot at glory for 'legends'? Guardiola still burning with desire
arts.sum: Pep Guardiola still faces a huge Manchester City rebuild but clouds lift as they head back to Wembley in the FA Cup, says chief football writer Phil McNulty.

arts.ttl: Inside Nico O'Reilly failed Chelsea January transfer as Man City learn Cole Palmer mistake
arts.sum: Nico O'Reilly assisted the Manchester City goals Erling Haaland and Omar Marmoush scored against Bournemouth in the FA Cup to secure their spot in the semi-finals

arts.ttl: Pep Guardiola hit with touchline ban and discovers Man City game he'll miss
arts.sum: Manchester City manager Pep Guardiola will not be on the touchline for his side's Premier League clash against Leicester on Wednesday after he was booked in their FA Cup win against Bournemouth

arts.ttl: Guardiola’s masterstroke pays off as O’Reilly energises Manchester City | Ben Fisher
arts.athr: Ben Fisher at the Vitality Stadium
arts.sum: Young substitute’s dynamic contribution turns tie around with commanding performance against Bournemouth The moment that flipped Manchester City’s FA Cup quarter-final at Bournemouth on its head initially went largely unnoticed. As players made a beeline for the tunnel and supporters headed into the concourses loaded with orders for beers, burgers and hot dogs, Pep Guardiola had issued an instruction that would ultimately define this match and earn City passage to a seventh successive semi-final. As everyone else headed in, his longtime fitness coach, Lorenzo Buenaventura, rushed on to the pitch with a single mission: prime Nico O’Reilly for business. The 20‑year-old from Failsworth, a few miles north of Manchester, ditched the tracksuit top and was quickly put through his paces. O’Reilly would not depart quite so quietly. Continue reading...
